transcript: powerless to escape. Beneath the Magic is a story about the pattern of Colombian and Latin American history from colonial times onward. This is a history that the author experienced firsthand. Gabriel Garcia Marquez grew up in a Colombia torn apart by civil conflict between its conservative and liberal political parties. He also lived in an autocratic Mexico and covered the 1958 Venezuelan coup d'etat as a journalist. But perhaps his biggest influences were his maternal grandparents. Nicolás Ricardo Marquez was a decorated veteran of the Thousand Days War, whose accounts of the rebellion against Colombia's conservative government led Gabriel Garcia Marquez to a socialist outlook.
